commit.message:
Fix DT001 unsafe DateTime to DateTimeOffset rewrites (#78)

Two-layer fix:

Layer 1 - Analyzer: suppress diagnostic for DateTime static members that
have no DateTimeOffset equivalent (Today, DaysInMonth, IsLeapYear,
SpecifyKind, FromBinary, FromOADate). These are either not actionable
or don't involve timezone concerns.

Layer 2 - Code fix: suppress fix for instance member chains where
downstream members don't exist on DateTimeOffset (e.g., DateTime.Now.Kind,
.ToBinary(), .ToShortDateString()), and for constructor overloads with no
matching DateTimeOffset constructor (e.g., new DateTime(y,m,d), new
DateTime(ticks)).

Uses DateTimeOffset.GetMembers() and InstanceConstructors with
HasImplicitConversion for definitive validation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Extract cookies from Zen, Firefox, Safari, and Chrome on startup
  when ZENDESK_COOKIE env var is not set
- Pick freshest cookie (latest expiry) when multiple browsers have it
- Try Zen/Firefox first to avoid macOS Keychain prompts from Chrome
- Auto-retry with refreshed cookie on 401 responses
- Update README and agent docs for new auth flow

commit.message:
feat: add task result retrieval for workflow executions (#72)

* feat: add task result retrieval for workflow executions

Add GET /platform/automation/v1/executions/{id}/tasks/{taskName}/result
support to surface the return value of individual tasks (e.g. the object
returned by a JavaScript task's default export function).

Changes:
- pkg/resources/workflow/execution.go: add GetTaskResult() method
- cmd/logs.go: add --result flag to 'logs wfe --task <name> --result'
- cmd/exec_workflows.go: add --show-results flag to 'exec workflow --wait'

Usage:
  # Fetch the return value of a specific task
  dtctl logs wfe <execution-id> --task <task-name> --result

  # Execute workflow and show all task return values on completion
  dtctl exec workflow <id> --wait --show-results

Fixes: without this, the only way to retrieve JavaScript task output was
to hit the automation REST API directly with curl/Bearer token.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: move task result retrieval to 'results wfe' command

- Remove --result flag from 'logs wfe' (logs = stdout/stderr; results
  = structured return values — semantically distinct)
- Add new 'dtctl results workflow-execution' command (alias: wfe) with
  required --task flag and -o json/yaml output support
- Add --show-results flag to 'exec workflow --wait' for convenience
- Update QUICK_START.md with both new commands

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: rename 'results wfe' to 'get wfe-task-result'

Per feedback in #71, move task result retrieval from the 'results' top-level
command group to 'get wfe-task-result', consistent with existing patterns
where 'get' is the verb for reading resources.

Before: dtctl results wfe <execution-id> --task <task-name>
After:  dtctl get wfe-task-result <execution-id> --task <task-name>
        dtctl get workflow-execution-task-result <execution-id> --task <task-name>

This mirrors the existing 'dtctl logs wfe' / 'dtctl get wfe' relationship
and maps directly to the API endpoint (.../result), leaving no ambiguity
between task results and task metadata.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: tighten code, fix docs, add tests

- Trim verbose godoc and Long description to match repo conventions
- Fix QUICK_START.md: update commands from 'results wfe' to 'get wfe-task-result'
- Add unit tests for GetTaskResult and ListTasks (table-driven, httptest)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address review issues in wfe-task-result feature

- Fix test indentation (was flush-left, now properly tabbed)
- Add agent envelope support to get wfe-task-result (enrichAgent + suggestions)
- Validate --show-results requires --wait (PreRunE check)
- Eliminate N+1 API calls in --show-results: use task.Result from
  ListTasks instead of calling GetTaskResult per task
- Move examples from Long to Example field in Cobra commands
- Fix help text grouping: place wfe-task-result near workflow-executions

* fix: add agent mode support for exec workflow and ListTasks result test

- In agent mode (-A), exec workflow now produces a single structured
  JSON envelope instead of mixing fmt.Printf with printer output
- Extract helper functions (execWorkflowWait, execWorkflowShowResults,
  execWorkflowAgent) for clarity and to separate human/agent code paths
- Add enrichAgent call with verb=exec, resource=workflow, plus
  suggestions and duration context
- Add TestListTasks_WithResults to verify Result field deserialization

commit.message:
Add baseline tests for insert/multi-insert. (#837)

Add baseline tests for `insert` and `multi_insert`, and address two
issues in multi-insert:

1. Relax the graph bootstrap trigger to activate even when some
intra-batch candidates are used. This handles a case where a large batch
is used with a small number of intra-batch candidates, leading to a
degenerate graph. Without this change, the candidates for the
`intra-batch-candides = 4` case fails to find the correct nearest
neighbors for the corner point searches.
2. Sort the aggregated backedges before adding them to the graph. This
change makes multi-insert repeatable regardless of the number of
threads. The non-determinism comes from the dynamic load balancing
mechanism meant to compensate for slow tasks. While the back edges
aggregated are the same, the order in which they are collected differs
depending on the order of task completion. Sorting them makes the result
repeatable. This *may* introduce a "downward" bias in the backedges, so
we could consider sorting them by hash rather than by value, but I don't
think it's likely to matter much.

## Baseline Tests
The new test file can be found in
`diskann/src/graph/test/cases/grid_insert.rs` and uses the baseline
caching infrastructure to add the following baselines:

| Grid | Single | Batch (all) | Batch (chunked) |
|------|--------|-------------|-----------------|
| 1D/100 | yes | ibc=none, ibc=all | no |
| 3D/5 | yes | ibc=none, ibc=all, ibc=max(4) | ibc=none, ibc=all |
| 4D/4 | yes | ibc=none, ibc=all, ibc=max(4) | ibc=none, ibc=all 

Where "Batch (chunked)" partitions the vectors to be inserted into
multiple mini-batches.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add date filter (#274)

This adds a `formatDate` filter which can be used with either the
`dateInput` component, like this:

```njk
{{ data.dateOfBirth | formatDate }}
``` 

which displays as:

> 7 January 1985

or you can use it with ISO formatted date strings, for example if you
use those in your session date defaults:

```
{{ "2026-02-15" | formatDate }}
```

There is also an option for including the day of the week:

```
{{ data.appointmentDate | formatDate({ includeDayOfWeek: true }}
```

> 3 Mar 2026


Code largely borrowed from the [`govukDate`
filter](https://github.com/x-govuk/govuk-prototype-filters/blob/main/lib/date.js#L141)
in the X-GOVUK prototype filters, but modified to work directly with the
date object rather than having to use `isoDateFromDateInput` filter
first.

Resolves #56

commit.message:
Add minimal wp-env PHPUnit application test scaffolding and developer-oriented docs (#178)

* Initial plan

* feat: add wp-env phpunit bootstrap docs and ci snippet

Co-authored-by: JUVOJustin <30726576+JUVOJustin@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: address review feedback on phpunit bootstrap docs

Co-authored-by: JUVOJustin <30726576+JUVOJustin@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: restructure testing guide with starlight steps and first test flow

Co-authored-by: JUVOJustin <30726576+JUVOJustin@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: rewrite testing guide as developer-oriented authoring walkthrough

Co-authored-by: JUVOJustin <30726576+JUVOJustin@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: tighten test examples in application testing guide

Co-authored-by: JUVOJustin <30726576+JUVOJustin@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: harden phpunit setup and activate CI test job

- Activate test-application CI job (was commented out)
- Remove deprecated phpunit.xml.dist attributes
- Add tests/php/ExampleTest.php as working smoke test
- Use container phpunit directly in test:php script (no Composer wrapper)
- Restore yoast/phpunit-polyfills as explicit dep (WordPress test bootstrap requirement)
- Remove phpunit/phpunit and wp-phpunit/wp-phpunit (provided by container and polyfills)
- Simplify bootstrap.php: drop non-wp-env fallback, fail fast with clear message
- Update testing docs: wp-env-exclusive constraint, tests-cli rationale, configuration notes

* ci: run application tests after static analysis passes

Exit early benefit: avoid spinning up Docker/wp-env when PHPStan
or lint already failed. Cache keys still resolve via needs: setup.

* docs: update github-actions doc with test-application job and stage structure

* chore: fix gitignore and distignore for phpunit artifacts

- Add phpunit.xml and .phpunit.result.cache to .gitignore
- Add phpunit.xml to .distignore
- Remove stale Cypress test entries from .gitignore

* docs: add plugin dependencies section to testing guide

* feat: add application-testing skill for writing PHPUnit tests

* chore: remove redundant namespace lookup guidance from testing skill

* docs: add application-testing skill to work-with-ai, add factory rollback note to testing

---------

commit.message:
docs: improve layout and readability of training method page

Why: Cards were cramming 5-6 per row on wide screens and diagnostic
tables had tight padding with no visual differentiation between rows.
Title referenced internal spec numbering that means nothing publicly.

Changes:
- Title: 'Methodical Spec Training After Spec10' -> 'Methodical DSL
  Training Methods'
- Card grids: triad fixed at 3 columns, decision/report/phase capped
  via minmax(280-300px) with max-width constraints
- Flow strip: raised minmax from 160px to 220px
- Card padding: increased from 1rem to 1.25rem
- Tables: added rounded border, alternating row backgrounds, hover
  highlights, more cell padding (0.9rem), uppercase headers
- Diagnostic matrix: added row-critical (red), row-warning (orange),
  row-info (blue) left-border indicators with color legend
- Responsive: single column below 900px, nowrap only on desktop

Test: Site rebuilds cleanly. All content preserved. No Spec10 reference
in title.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
